title: empetrum nigrum subsp. albidum
intro: Empetrum nigrum subsp. albidum, also known as white crowberry, is a subspecies of the crowberry plant that belongs to the heath family, Ericaceae. This low-growing, evergreen shrub is native to the Arctic tundra and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The white crowberry has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and possessing immunomodulatory effects. It can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies to treat various conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
